sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 10

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section IX, List of Answers on Terminology 10:1
items.html:
(to the question) Botzina de Kardinuta [lamp of darkness]
Botzina means a lamp; Kardinuta means darkness. That is, a lamp that shines from the discernment of the darkness within it. This alludes to the Masach [screen] corrected in the point of Malchut, which bears within it the power of the Tzimtzum [restriction]; with this Masach she raises Ohr Chozer [Returning Light] and extends and clothes all the Lights in the Partzuf, as is known. The root of the Botzina de Kardinuta is from the discernment of the point under the Yesod of ZA, called Ateret HaYesod [the crown of Yesod], as is known. However, at the time of the Gadlut [greatness] of ZA, the place of the Botzina de Kardinuta is discerned at the place of the opening of the Chazeh [chest] of ZA. The reason is that at the time of Gadlut, CHaGaT rise and become CHaBaD, and NHY become CHaGaT, and new NHY come out in him. Then Yesod is found to have risen and become the upper third of Tifferet, and the point of Malchut, which cleaves to the Yesod, is found there at the ending of the Yesod, standing at the opening of the Chazeh.
